item.title: Novo Holdings Invests in Superconductors Amid EU Push for Fusion
item.description: Novo Holdings, the controlling shareholder of Novo Nordisk A/S, is topping up an investment in a Danish company specialized in superconductor technology, as part of an effort to accelerate new energy resources in Europe.

item.title: Battery Recycler Ascend Elements Files for Bankruptcy
item.description: The climate tech company boomed during the Biden years but has struggled amid a drop in EV interest.

item.title: Wildfires Race Across US as Drought Spans Half the Nation
item.description: An unusually hot, snowless winter has fueled a fast start to the spring fire season across large swaths of the western, central and southeastern US, disrupting businesses and upending daily life.

item.title: How China Stands to Benefit From the Iran War
item.description: As countries turn to renewable sources to bolster their energy security, a lot of that equipment will come from China.

item.title: El Niño Set to Form by August With Rising Heat and Crop Risks
item.description: A planet-heating El Niño is favored to emerge by August, with rising odds of an unusually powerful event that threatens record-breaking temperatures during the Northern Hemisphere’s summer.

item.title: EU Eyes Flexibility on Methane Rules to Avert Gas Supply Risk
item.description: The European Union will soon offer flexibility to companies on both penalties and tracing energy imports to help ensure that its landmark law to tackle methane emissions in fossil fuel imports doesn’t put its supply at risk.

item.title: Emperor Penguins Declared Endangered as Antarctica Loses Ice
item.description: The birds face a major threat to their survival as climate change shrinks the sea ice they rely on for breeding.

item.title: Iran War’s Fuel Shock Will Reshape the Future of Energy
item.description: Will Asia return to coal, or will a new renewables future emerge from the Iran crisis? David Fickling and Akshat Rathi discuss.

item.title: Lee Zeldin Touts EPA Rollbacks at Climate Denial Conference
item.description: The EPA administrator urged attendees to “celebrate vindication” amid the Trump administration’s deregulatory push.

item.title: India Withdraws Bid to Host 2028 UN Climate Talks
item.description: India has withdrawn its bid to host the 2028 United Nations-backed climate summit, a setback for the diplomatic process to combat global warming under the Paris Agreement.

item.title: US EV Fast-Charging Network Grows Amid High Gas Prices
item.description: From truck stops to cities, infrastructure is spreading across the US.

item.title: US Charging Networks Race to Keep Up as Gas Prices Boost EVs
item.description: Despite a rapid buildup, the US still has more EVs per charger than other rich countries.

item.title: EDF Pledges €240 Million Support for Heat Pumps, Electric Trucks
item.description: Electricite de France SA pledged €240 million ($280 million) in investment and funding for households and businesses to boost the adoption of heat pumps and electric trucks, and help firms set up power-hungry facilities.

item.title: UK Electric Vehicle Sales Rise to Record High Amid Fuel Price Rise
item.description: High gas prices are sparking more interest, but it remains to be seen whether EV sales will continue to surge.

item.title: Battery Giant CATL Taps Zijin Founder as Advisor for Mining Arm
item.description: Top electric-vehicle battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. has tapped the founder and ex-chairman of Zijin Mining Group Co. Ltd. as an advisor for its growing mining business, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India Monsoon Rains Seen Below Normal, Private Forecaster Says
item.description: Monsoon rains in India are likely to be below the long-term average this year, as an expected El Niño weather pattern during the four-month season could reduce precipitation, according to a private forecaster.

item.title: States Are Weighing Whether to Soften Climate Laws Amid High Energy Prices
item.description: Lawmakers are grappling with rising gas prices, but experts warn rolling back regulations won’t necessarily help cut bills.

item.title: State Climate Laws Targeted Around US as Iran War Spikes Gas Prices
item.description: Experts say the laws have little impact on gasoline costs and are the cheapest way to meet rising electricity demand.

item.title: Russia Says Dagestan Region Sees Blackouts After Severe Storms
item.description: Heavy rains, wet snow and strong winds damaged some power facilities in the Russian region of Dagestan, leaving dozens of villages without electricity, local officials said.

item.title: Sydney’s E-Bike Boom Hits the Brakes as Injuries Rise
item.description: Serious injuries are on the rise, and authorities are increasingly concerned about high-powered models.

item.title: US Seeks to Streamline Offshore Management in Regulator Overhaul
item.description: The US Interior Department said it’s merging two oversight agencies to create a Marine Minerals Administration and move toward a “more modern, coordinated approach” to managing offshore resources, including critical minerals.

item.title: What a Clean Cookstove Company’s Bankruptcy Means for Carbon Markets
item.description: Prices on Corsia, a marketplace for airlines, have fallen in the months following Koko’s collapse.

item.title: France Plans to Award 10 Offshore and Floating Wind Projects by Early 2027
item.description: France plans to hold a big tender for 10 offshore wind projects, and announce winners by late this year or early 2027, as part of its green push.

item.title: Dry Winter Saps Norway’s Hydropower Potential
item.description: The country acts as Europe’s battery, but it doesn’t have enough charge after below-normal snowfall.

item.title: Neoen to Build France’s Largest Battery Amid Strained Power Grid
item.description: Renewable energy developer Neoen will build France’s largest battery, helping to store rising solar and wind generation and ease strain on the country’s grid.

item.title: A Winter Without Snow Depletes Europe’s Clean Energy Reservoir
item.description: Hydropower, a key export from Norway, is being tested by the country’s driest winter in years, pushing electricity prices higher.

item.title: How a Company Collapse Is Denting Trust in Carbon Markets – Again
item.description: This year was supposed to be a turning point for carbon markets, with the United Nations’ long-delayed country-to-country trading system coming into force and airlines preparing to enter a mandatory program to offset their emissions.

item.title: Climate Tech is Making Europe More Resilient to Energy Shocks
item.description: The world is facing a second energy crisis in less than five years. Is it better prepared this time? Investor Aurore Belfrage joins the Zero podcast to discuss.

item.title: Fund Sees ‘Significant’ Opportunity in India’s New Climate Plan
item.description: India’s new emissions-reduction plans offer significant investment opportunities in sectors related to grid upgrades and storage batteries, according to one of the country’s largest climate-focused funds.

item.title: The US Data Center Boom Is Hitting a Transformer Crunch
item.description: More than half the US data centers planned for this year are expected to be delayed.
